meanings.definition: the act of creating written works
examples: writing was a form of therapy for him
it was a matter of disputed authorship

meanings.definition: the work of a writer; anything expressed in letters of the alphabet (especially when considered from the point of view of style and effect)
examples: the writing in her novels is excellent
that editorial was a fine piece of writing

meanings.definition: (usually plural) the collected work of an author
examples: the idea occurs with increasing frequency in Hemingway's writings

meanings.definition: letters or symbols that are written or imprinted on a surface to represent the sounds or words of a language
examples: he turned the paper over so the writing wouldn't show
the doctor's writing was illegible

meanings.definition: the activity of putting something in written form
examples: she did the thinking while he did the writing
She composed a poem
He wrote four novels
Please write to me every week

meanings.definition: have (one's written work) issued for publication
examples: How many books did Georges Simenon write?
She published 25 books during her long career
Write her soon, please!
He wrote that he would be coming soon
Beethoven composed nine symphonies

meanings.definition: mark or trace on a surface
examples: The artist wrote Chinese characters on a big piece of white paper
Russian is written with the Cyrillic alphabet
boot-up instructions are written on the hard disk

meanings.definition: write or name the letters that comprise the conventionally accepted form of (a word or part of a word)
examples: He spelled the word wrong in this letter

meanings.definition: create code, write a computer program
examples: She writes code faster than anybody else
